command: WITHHELD_FOR_SAFETY - mshta variant: inline JScript/VBScript protocol invocation from PowerShell
explanation: Captures the pattern of invoking mshta with inline script protocol content (e.g., jscript/vbscript) directly from PowerShell, avoiding disk-based HTA artifacts.
rationale_steps: PowerShell launches mshta with a script protocol payload rather than a file path
Execution occurs in-memory within the signed host process
Diskless execution can reduce on-disk IoCs and evade simple file-based controls
Monitor for mshta with command lines containing script protocol tokens spawned by PowerShell
Block or constrain mshta usage and enforce AMSI instrumentation where possible

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: No concrete PowerShell/mshta command is provided—only a descriptive placeholder—so it is not parseable or runnable as-is.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Invoking mshta with inline JScript/VBScript is plausible for execution but maps to ATT&CK T1218.005 (Mshta) rather than T1216’s system script proxies, so it is misaligned with the claimed technique.

llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The described technique (mshta with inline jscript/vbscript protocol from PowerShell) is a real and documented LOLBin/proxy execution pattern, but it maps more precisely to T1218.005 (Signed Binary Proxy Execution: Mshta) than to T1216 (System Script Proxy Execution), which focuses on signed VBS/script files like PubPrn.vbs rather than mshta binary invocation.
